key_insight: A one-step flow matching model in latent space can generate high-resolution, path-dependent plastic stress fields across all time steps with much lower sampling cost than diffusion-based methods.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate simulation for complex geometries and loading histories in computational mechanics, making plasticity-aware field prediction more practical for design and analysis.
method: Transformer-based flow matching operates in VAE latent space to model full stress-field sequences as a video generation problem.
A non-Gaussian source distribution is used to reduce crossing of conditional transport paths during training.
Token-level loading embeddings plus two auxiliary networks improve conditioning on geometry and loading-unloading paths.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate high-resolution generation even with limited training data, and claims a 6–7× CPU speedup over FEM plus roughly two orders of magnitude speedup on consumer GPUs.
watch_item: Performance depends on the learned latent representation and training coverage of loading paths/geometries, so rare or highly out-of-distribution plastic responses may still be challenging.
paper_title: One-Step Flow Matching for Generative Modeling of Path-Dependent Physical Fields
